findings: Heart size is within normal limits. The lungs are clear bilaterally. No focal consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. The mediastinal contours are normal. No acute osseous abnormality.

The cardiac silhouette is enlarged with a cardiothoracic ratio exceeding 0.5. Pulmonary vascularity appears mildly prominent. No focal consolidation. No pleural effusion. The mediastinal contours are otherwise unremarkable.

Airspace opacity in the right lower lobe consistent with consolidation. Blunting of the right costophrenic angle suggesting small right pleural effusion. The left lung is clear. Heart size is within normal limits. No pneumothorax.

Fractures of the right 4th through 7th ribs along the posterior axillary line. Small right apical pneumothorax with visible visceral pleural line. Subcutaneous emphysema in the right lateral chest wall. Underlying lung parenchyma is otherwise clear. Heart size normal. No pleural effusion.

Portable AP technique with limited inspiration. Endotracheal tube with tip approximately 4 cm above the carina in satisfactory position. Nasogastric tube tip projects over the stomach. Diffuse bilateral airspace opacities more prominent in the lower zones. Small bilateral pleural effusions cannot be excluded given supine positioning. Cardiac silhouette is difficult to assess on AP projection.
